problem text: Randomly choose two different prime numbers from the range $1$ to $20$, inclusive. Determine the probability that their sum results in an even number.
problem solution: The primes not exceeding $20$ are $2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17,$ and $19$, adding up to $8$ numbers. There are $8 \cdot 7 = 56$ possible ordered pairs for selecting $2$ numbers. The sum is even if $2$ is not chosen. Hence, there are $7 \cdot 6 = 42$ ways to select two primes excluding $2$. Thus, the probability is $\dfrac{42}{56} = \dfrac{3}{4}$.
problem hint: The primes not exceeding $20$ are $2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13, 17,$ and $19$, adding up to $8$ numbers.
